docs.title: Home-Manager Build Failures
docs.text:
Symptom: Build errors when running just apply

Solutions:

  1. Validate flake syntax: 

    nix flake check

  2. Check for syntax errors in modules: 

    # Validate specific configuration
nix build .#homeConfigurations.hm-x86_64-linux.activationPackage --dry-run

  3. Clear old generations and retry: 

    just gc-force
just apply

docs.title: Architecture Mismatch
docs.text:
Symptom: Build errors mentioning unsupported architecture

Solution: Verify correct platform detection: 

# Check detected architecture
echo "OS: $(just OS_TYPE), Arch: $(just SYSTEM_ARCH)"

# Manually specify architecture if needed
just apply x86_64-linux    # 64-bit Linux
just apply aarch64-linux   # ARM64 Linux
just apply x86_64-darwin   # Intel macOS
just apply aarch64-darwin  # Apple Silicon macOS

docs.title: Dynamically Linked Executables Failing
docs.text:
Symptom: "No such file or directory" errors when running downloaded binaries

Explanation: This configuration includes nix-ld to run non-Nix dynamically linked executables.

Solution: If specific libraries are missing, add them to programs.nix-ld.libraries in configuration.nix: 

programs.nix-ld = {
  enable = true;
  libraries = with pkgs; [
    # Add missing libraries here
    stdenv.cc.cc
    zlib
    # ... other libraries
  ];
};

docs.title: Shell Not Updating
docs.text:
Symptom: Changes not reflected in shell after home-manager switch

Solution: 

# Restart terminal or reload shell
exec fish

# Or source the new profile
source ~/.config/fish/config.fish

docs.title: Permission Issues
docs.text:
Symptom: Permission denied errors when running commands

Solution: Ensure user is in required groups: 

# Check current groups
groups

# Add user to docker group (if using Docker compatibility)
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER

# Add user to wheel group (for sudo)
sudo usermod -aG wheel $USER

# Logout and login for group changes to take effect

This shows: - Store metrics and disk usage - Configuration status - Binary cache status - Garbage collection recommendations - Quick performance test results

docs.title: Clean Installation (Reset)
docs.text:
If all else fails, perform a clean installation:

# Remove home-manager completely
just uninstall-home-manager

# Remove all dotfiles and configurations
just purge-local-configs

# Fresh installation from scratch
just bootstrap

Warning: This will remove all home-manager configurations. Make sure to backup any important customizations first.

docs.title: Agent Policy Contract System
docs.text: Nix module system을 DDD/OOP IoC 컨테이너로 활용한 에이전트 정책 관리 아키텍처.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: CLAUDE.md의 텍스트 기반 가드레일을 빌드 타임에 검증되는 코드 기반 정책으로 전환한다. 각 provider(Claude, Gemini, Codex)는 공통 contract를 구현하고, mixin으로 필요한 capability를 선택적으로 조합한다.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
modules/agents/
├── policy-contract.nix       # Interface: option type 선언
├── policy-assertions.nix     # Build-time contract validation
├── policy-hook-adapters.nix  # Provider별 hook format adapter (SSoT)
├── policy-assembler.nix      # IoC assembler (entry point)
├── policy-provider-hooks.nix # Base + policy hook merge helper
├── policy-phase-gate.nix     # (E) State Machine Adapter
├── policy-path-guard.nix     # Security Path Guard
├── policy-strategy-lint.nix  # (F) Strategy Linter + Peer Review Gate
├── policy-reasoning-trace.nix# (A) Reasoning Trace 분리
├── policy-async-handshake.nix# (B) Async Sub-Agent Handshake
└── policy-live-oracle.nix    # (D) Live Verification Oracle

docs.title: Contract (Interface)
docs.text:
modules/agents/policy-contract.nix에서 agentPolicy.providers.<name> submodule로 정의:

Option Group Purpose Key Fields reasoning 사고 과정 분리 전략 mode (silent/verbose/log-only), traceDir async 비동기 협업 enabled, backgroundTasks, handshakeProtocol oracle 실시간 검증 enabled, healthChecks, streamAnalysis phases 상태 머신 강제 enforced, stateDir, gatedTools strategyLint 전략 문서 검증 enabled, requiredSections, peerReviewProvider

Hook 렌더링 메타데이터(format, timeout)는 contract가 아닌 내부 agentPolicy._providerRuntime.<name>.hooks namespace에 둔다. format enum은 hook-adapters.nix의 adapter 이름에서 자동 파생되므로, 새 포맷을 추가할 때는 hook-adapters.nix만 수정하면 된다.

docs.title: Claude (Orchestrator)
docs.text:
agentPolicy.providers.claude = {
  enable = true;
  reasoning.mode = "silent";
  oracle.enabled = true;
  oracle.healthChecks = [{ command = "nix flake check --no-build"; ... }];
  phases.enforced = true;
  strategyLint.enabled = true;
  strategyLint.peerReviewProvider = "gemini";
};

Mixins: phase-gate, path-guard, strategy-lint, reasoning-trace, live-oracle

docs.title: Gemini (Researcher/Critic)
docs.text:
agentPolicy.providers.gemini = {
  enable = true;
  reasoning.mode = "verbose";
  async.enabled = true;
  async.backgroundTasks = ["strategy-review" "blindspot-audit" "impact-analysis"];
};

Mixins: path-guard, reasoning-trace, async-handshake

docs.title: Codex (Logic Verifier)
docs.text:
agentPolicy.providers.codex = {
  enable = true;
  reasoning.mode = "log-only";
};

Mixins: path-guard, reasoning-trace

docs.title: Pattern Mapping
docs.text: OOP/DDD Nix Module System Interface mkOption type declarations in contract.nix Implementation Provider module sets agentPolicy.providers.<name> values Assertion config.assertions — fails nix build on violation Mixin imports — selective capability composition IoC/DI Module system auto-wires option producers to consumers Adapter hook-adapters.nix — transforms hooks to provider format

docs.title: Assertions (Build-time Validation)
docs.text:
nix build 시 다음을 자동 검증:

  1. strategyLint.enabled → peerReviewProvider가 반드시 존재
  2. peerReviewProvider → 실존하는 provider 이름인지 확인
  3. phases.enforced → gatedTools가 비어있지 않은지
  4. oracle.enabled → healthChecks가 최소 1개
  5. async.enabled → backgroundTasks가 최소 1개
  6. strategyLint.enabled → requiredSections가 비어있지 않은지

docs.title: Hook Generation Flow
docs.text:
Contract options
    |  set by provider modules
    v
Mixins (consume options, produce _hooks)
    |  { mixin-name.provider-name = { event, matcher, script } }
    v
hook-adapters.nix (format conversion)
    |  claude: settings.json / gemini: settings.json / codex: config.toml
    v
assembler.nix (assembler)
    |  produces provider-native assembled hooks
    v
provider-runtime.nix
    |  merges with base hooks and renders settings files
    v
modules/agents/sync-mutable-config.nix (injection)
    |  deep-merge into mutable settings file
    v
Provider CLI reads settings on startup

docs.title: Adding a New Provider
docs.text:
1. Create modules/agents/<name>.nix
  2. Set agentPolicy.providers.<name> = { enable = true; ... }
  3. Set agentPolicy._providerRuntime.<name>.hooks = { format = "..."; timeout = 5; }
  4. Import desired mixins (or rely on assembler.nix which imports all)
  5. Add format case to hook-adapters.nix if needed
  6. Run nix build — assertions validate your contract

docs.title: Adding a New Mixin
docs.text:
1. Create modules/agents/policy-<name>.nix
  2. Read from config.agentPolicy.providers (filter by relevant options)
  3. Write to config.agentPolicy._hooks.<mixin-name> (internal hook registry)
  4. Add import to modules/agents/policy-assembler.nix
  5. Run nix build — hook automatically appears in provider settings

docs.title: Claude Code Integration
docs.text: Claude Code operates as the orchestrator in a multi-provider agent architecture. This document covers the full integration: configuration sync, hook pipeline, slash commands, sub-agents, and the policy contract system.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Claude Code (orchestrator)
  ├─ Gemini CLI (researcher/critic) — async FIFO handshake
  ├─ Codex (logic verifier) — log-only reasoning traces
  └─ cli-proxy-api (localhost:4001) — unified auth + cost logging + routing

Configuration is managed declaratively through Nix: - Static files (commands, agents, skills, hooks) are symlinked via home.file - Dynamic settings (permissions, MCP servers, hooks) are deep-merged into ~/.claude.json and ~/.claude/settings.json via activation scripts - Policy-generated hooks are produced by the policy-*.nix mixin modules in modules/agents/ at build time and merged with base hooks

docs.title: Configuration Sync
docs.text:
When you run just apply, home-manager:

  1. Symlinks dotfiles/claude/{commands,agents,skills,hooks} to ~/.claude/
  2. Generates MCP server config from modules/agents/agents-mcp.nix (SSoT) and merges into ~/.claude.json
  3. Generates settings from dotfiles/claude/settings.json + policy hooks and merges into ~/.claude/settings.json
  4. Preserves runtime data (projects, tips, OAuth tokens) through jq deep-merge

Key implementation files: - modules/agents/sync-mutable-config.nix — mkJsonSync (deep-merge) and mkFileCopy (overwrite with backup) - modules/agents/mcp-adapters.nix — transforms canonical MCP definitions to Claude/Gemini/Codex formats

docs.title: Hook Pipeline
docs.text: All hooks are shell scripts that read JSON from stdin and return exit codes: 0 (allow), 1 (retry), 2 (block).

docs.title: UserPromptSubmit
docs.text: Hook Purpose complexity-router.sh Injects S/M/L classification prompt on first turn

docs.title: PreToolUse
docs.text: Matcher Hook Purpose Bash cmd-guard.sh Blocks destructive shell commands Bash branch-guard.sh Protects main/production branches Write\|Edit\|NotebookEdit phase-gate-claude.sh * Contract-generated phase enforcement Write\|Edit\|NotebookEdit strategy-lint-claude.sh * Contract-generated strategy validation Write\|Edit\|Read path-guard-claude.sh * Contract-generated from global.sensitivePatterns Agent cost-gate.sh Blocks at >80% context window

docs.title: PostToolUse
docs.text: Matcher Hook Purpose Bash proxy-route.sh Logs tool usage to cli-proxy-api Bash escalation-gate.sh Tracks failures, auto-stash at 3rd, escalates Bash test-feedback.sh Parses test output, extracts top errors Write\|Edit escalation-gate.sh Same failure tracking for file mutations Write\|Edit auto-lint.sh Auto-format after writes Write\|Edit\|NotebookEdit live-oracle-claude.sh * Contract-generated runtime health checks all reasoning-trace-claude.sh * Contract-generated reasoning separation

docs.title: Stop
docs.text:
Hook Purpose agent-notify.sh claude macOS notification on session end

* Generated by Agent Policy Contract system at nix build time

docs.title: Slash Commands
docs.text: Command File Purpose /commit commands/commit.md SRP-based change separation and commit /create-pull-request commands/create-pull-request.md Analyze changes, generate PR /scaffold commands/scaffold.md KISS skeleton code generation /code-enhance commands/code-enhance.md Quality, performance, security analysis /debug-system commands/debug-system.md Systematic root cause analysis /blog-korean commands/blog-korean.md 8-step Korean tech blog workflow /blog-refine commands/blog-refine.md Diagnose and improve existing blog posts /test-doc-korean commands/test-doc-korean.md Korean test case documentation /reset-memory commands/reset-memory.md Clear conversation memory

docs.title: Sub-Agents
docs.text:
Seven specialized agents in dotfiles/claude/agents/:

Agent Role Key Tools architect Technical planning and design Read, Write, Glob, Grep implementer Code implementation matching existing patterns Read, Write, Edit, Bash tester Framework-adaptive test suite creation Read, Write, Edit, Bash reviewer Security, performance, quality analysis Read, Grep, Glob refactorer Code optimization and cleanup Read, Write, Edit, Bash researcher Gemini-powered research and web exploration Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch cross-validator GPT-powered independent second opinions Bash, Read, Grep, Glob

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
Three reusable skills in dotfiles/claude/skills/:

Skill Trigger Purpose architectural-planning plan, design, architecture, integrate Feature design, migration planning, integration code-implementation implement, write code, add feature, fix bug Pattern-matching code changes test-development test, coverage, unit test, E2E Framework-adaptive test suites

docs.title: Agent Policy Contract
docs.text:
The policy system (policy-*.nix modules under modules/agents/) uses Nix modules as a DDD-style IoC container. Claude's contract implementation enables:

  • Silent reasoning — chain-of-thought logged to /tmp/agent-traces/claude/, only decisions shown
  • Phase gate — Write/Edit blocked until L-complexity strategy is approved
  • Strategy lint — requires pre-mortem, tradeoffs, peer-review sections; gates on Gemini review
  • Live oracle — nix flake check --no-build runs after every file mutation
  • Path guard — sensitive file patterns from agentPolicy.global.sensitivePatterns

Full documentation: agent-policy-contract.md

docs.title: Add/Remove Permissions
docs.text:
Edit dotfiles/claude/settings.json: 

{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Read(*)", "Glob(*)", "YourNewTool(*)"]
  }
}

Then apply: just apply

docs.title: Add MCP Servers
docs.text:
Edit modules/agents/agents-mcp.nix (single source of truth): 

programs.mcp.servers.my-server = {
  command = "npx";
  args = ["-y" "@example/mcp-server@latest"];
};

The adapter (modules/agents/mcp-adapters.nix) automatically converts to Claude/Gemini/Codex formats.

docs.title: Add a Hook
docs.text: For one-off hooks, add to dotfiles/claude/settings.json. For policy-driven hooks, create or modify a policy-*.nix mixin module in modules/agents/ — it will be generated and merged at build time.

docs.title: Hook Pipeline Reference
docs.text: Note: Generated hooks — Hooks marked as "Generated" in the table below do not exist in the repository source tree. They are produced by the Agent Policy Contract system during nix build and merged into provider settings at activation time (just apply). Hand-editing these scripts directly has no effect; regenerate them by modifying the relevant policy options and running just apply.

docs.title: How Hooks Work
docs.text:
Claude Code (and compatible providers) execute hook scripts at specific lifecycle events. Each hook receives a JSON payload on stdin describing the event context. Scripts communicate intent through their exit code:

Exit Code Meaning 0 Allow — proceed normally 1 Retry — surface the hook's stdout to the model as feedback 2 Block — abort the tool call; show stdout as the reason

Hooks read the event payload with a pattern like:

input=$(cat)
tool_name=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_name // empty')
tool_input=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input // empty')

The exact JSON schema varies by event type. PreToolUse events include tool_name and tool_input. PostToolUse events include tool_output. UserPromptSubmit includes the raw prompt.

docs.title: Full Pipeline
docs.text:
Event Matcher Hook Script Purpose Generated UserPromptSubmit (all prompts) complexity-router.sh Classify prompt as S/M/L and inject the complexity tier into the conversation context No PreToolUse Bash cmd-guard.sh Block destructive shell commands (rm -rf /, git push --force to main, etc.) No PreToolUse Bash branch-guard.sh Prevent commits and force-pushes to protected branches No PreToolUse Write, Edit, Read phase-gate-claude.sh Enforce the RESEARCH → STRATEGY → EXECUTION state machine; block writes until strategy is approved Yes* PreToolUse Write, Edit, Read path-guard-claude.sh Provider-specific path guard generated from the policy contract Yes* PreToolUse Write, Edit, NotebookEdit strategy-lint-claude.sh Validate that a strategy document containing required sections (pre-mortem, tradeoffs, peer-review) exists before allowing writes Yes* PreToolUse Agent cost-gate.sh Block sub-agent spawning when context usage exceeds 80% No PostToolUse Bash proxy-route.sh Log token costs via cli-proxy-api; route to the appropriate provider endpoint No PostToolUse Bash escalation-gate.sh Track retry count; escalate to human after repeated failures No PostToolUse Bash test-feedback.sh Parse test output and return structured feedback to the model on failure No PostToolUse Write, Edit escalation-gate.sh Same escalation tracking applied to file write failures No PostToolUse Write, Edit auto-lint.sh Run the appropriate linter (alejandra, ruff, prettier, etc.) and return lint output as feedback No PostToolUse Write, Edit, NotebookEdit live-oracle-claude.sh Run health checks (e.g. nix flake check) after mutations and block progression if they fail Yes* PostToolUse (all tools) reasoning-trace-claude.sh Capture reasoning traces to /tmp/agent-traces (silent mode for Claude) Yes* Stop (session end) agent-notify.sh claude Send a macOS notification when the Claude session ends No

Marked Yes in the Generated column means the script is produced by the Agent Policy Contract system at nix build time. These scripts are not hand-written; they are rendered from the policy-.nix mixin modules in modules/agents/ and written to ~/.claude/settings.json via home.activation.syncClaudeSettings.*

docs.title: Agent Policy Contract Hooks
docs.text:
The four generated hooks correspond to policy mixins:

Hook Mixin Source Policy Option phase-gate-claude.sh modules/agents/policy-phase-gate.nix agentPolicy.providers.claude.phases.enforced path-guard-claude.sh modules/agents/policy-path-guard.nix (always active when provider is enabled) strategy-lint-claude.sh modules/agents/policy-strategy-lint.nix agentPolicy.providers.claude.strategyLint.enabled live-oracle-claude.sh modules/agents/policy-live-oracle.nix agentPolicy.providers.claude.oracle.enabled reasoning-trace-claude.sh modules/agents/policy-reasoning-trace.nix agentPolicy.providers.claude.reasoning.mode

Changing any of these options and running just apply regenerates the corresponding scripts. Violations of contract invariants (e.g. enabling strategyLint without setting peerReviewProvider) cause nix build to fail with an assertion error before any scripts are written.

docs.title: Hook Location
docs.text: All hand-written hook scripts live in dotfiles/claude/hooks/. The generated hooks are assembled into ~/.claude/settings.json under the hooks key during home.activation. The base settings.json in dotfiles/claude/settings.json defines the static hooks; policy hooks are deep-merged on top per event array.

docs.title: Statusline Hooks
docs.text:
Two additional scripts manage the Claude terminal status line and are not part of the event pipeline:

Script Purpose statusline.sh 2-line status bar showing phase, complexity, turn count, and context subagent-statusline.sh Per-row status for sub-agent panels

These are sourced by the terminal environment rather than invoked as hook scripts.

Package Purpose bat cat with syntax highlighting jq JSON processor moreutils sponge and other shell utilities ripgrep Fast recursive search fd Fast find alternative tree Directory tree display curl HTTP client openssl TLS utilities lazygit Git TUI ghalint GitHub Actions linter bfg-repo-cleaner Git history cleaner zellij Terminal multiplexer fastfetch System info display asciinema + asciinema-agg Terminal recording redli Redis CLI sops Secret operations age File encryption ssh-to-age SSH key to age key converter git-crypt Git-integrated encryption git-filter-repo Git history rewriting gh GitHub CLI gnupg GPG toolchain

macOS additions: pngpaste, terminal-notifier Linux additions: xclip, google-authenticator (Linux, including WSL)

docs.title: Shell Environment (fish.nix, git.nix, editor.nix, etc.)
docs.text:
These modules configure programs via programs.<name> rather than home.packages. Key programs:

Module Program fish.nix fish shell with abbreviations and environment git.nix git with delta pager editor.nix Neovim (LazyVim distribution) fzf.nix fzf with fish keybindings direnv.nix direnv + nix-direnv yazi.nix yazi terminal file manager

docs.title: JetBrains IDEs — modules/packages/jetbrains.hm.nix
docs.text: JetBrains IDEs are declared in a dedicated module. The exact set may vary; check the file for the current list.

docs.title: AI Agent Infrastructure — modules/agents/
docs.text:
Package / Service Module Purpose claude-code apps.nix Claude Code CLI binary gemini-cli apps.nix Gemini CLI binary codex apps.nix OpenAI Codex CLI binary cli-proxy-api agents/agents-proxy.nix OAuth proxy; routes requests to all three providers MCP servers agents/agents-mcp.nix SSoT MCP server definitions, adapted per provider

cli-proxy-api is managed as a launchd service on macOS (auto-start on login). On Linux it runs as a user systemd service.
